verdict_reasoning: EREG is an EGF-family ligand synthesized as a single-pass type I transmembrane proligand (proEREG) that transits the plasma membrane and is cleaved by ADAM sheddases to release the soluble mature ligand that activates EGFR/ERBB4. The membrane-anchored pro-form is transient but real, supports juxtacrine signaling, and is documented at the cell surface; however, the canonical biological actor is the shed soluble ligand. This matches the transient-TM-precursor pattern: contextual via dual_localization rather than secreted_only.
